context: Tests whether the agent can implement a Turbo Stream live-update for a notification badge using broadcast_replace_to, turbo_stream_from subscription, and proper controller respond_to blocks, following the rails-frontend-hotwire skill.

checklist.name: broadcast_replace_to implemented
checklist.description: app/models/notification.rb implements broadcast_badge_update using broadcast_replace_to (or Turbo::StreamsChannel.broadcast_replace_to) with a stream name tied to the user

checklist.name: Badge partial created
checklist.description: app/views/shared/_notification_badge.html.erb exists and renders the unread count using a dom_id or fixed id (e.g. notification_badge)

checklist.name: turbo_stream_from in layout
checklist.description: app/views/layouts/application.html.erb includes turbo_stream_from with the correct stream name (e.g. current_user or notifications_user_#{current_user.id})

checklist.name: Controller responds to turbo_stream
checklist.description: NotificationsController#index has a respond_to block that handles both :html and :turbo_stream formats

checklist.name: Stream targets correct DOM id
checklist.description: The broadcast targets the same DOM element id used in the badge partial (consistent dom_id or string id)

checklist.name: No JavaScript files added
checklist.description: The solution does not add any .js or .stimulus files — only Turbo Streams

checklist.name: frozen_string_literal in Ruby files
checklist.description: All modified or created .rb files include # frozen_string_literal: true

checklist.name: Unread count passed to broadcast
checklist.description: The broadcast_badge_update method (or the partial it renders) correctly passes/displays the current unread count

checklist.name: authenticate_user! preserved
checklist.description: The controller still has before_action :authenticate_user! and the action still sets @notifications and @unread_count
